overview: This is a Scorpion-tailed spider (Arachnura genus), easily identified by the long, yellow, leaf-like appendage extending from its abdomen. Despite looking intimidating, these spiders are harmless to humans, as they possess no venomous stinger in their tail and rarely bite. The tail is found only on females and is used to mimic dead leaves or scorpion tails to deter predators. They are a type of orb-weaver spider that hangs on its web to catch insects. This is a Scorpion-tailed spider (Arachnura genus), easily identified by the long, yellow, leaf-like appendage extending from its abdomen.
Despite looking intimidating, these spiders are harmless to humans, as they possess no venomous stinger in their tail and rarely bite.
The tail is found only on females and is used to mimic dead leaves or scorpion tails to deter predators.
They are a type of orb-weaver spider that hangs on its web to catch insects.
